# Existing Tools/Plugins for LLM-Assisted Estimation
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

> Dimension 5 of 8 | See [../citations.md](../citations.md) for full source details

## Key Finding

The landscape spans five categories: dedicated AI estimation platforms, Jira plugins, AI sprint planning assistants, productivity measurement platforms, and academic prototypes. **No tool currently adjusts estimates based on expected AI coding assistant usage.** The gap between "AI that estimates work" and "estimation that accounts for AI doing the work" remains unfilled.

---

## Category 1: Dedicated AI Estimation Platforms

| Tool | Method | Claimed Accuracy | Integrations | Ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Devtimate | NLU from uploaded requirements | 85-90% initial, 95%+ after review | Jira, Asana, Excel | [63] |
| CostGPT.ai | 2,000+ project history + ML | 80%+ | Export only | [64] |
| Quanter/QuanterIA | LLM-based Function Point Analysis (IFPUG/NESMA) | ISO-compliant | Jira | [65] |
| EstimateAI | ChatGPT-integrated task estimation | Not disclosed | Web app | Listed for sale on Flippa |
| Baseliner AI | Reinforcement learning + 3-point estimation | Claims 95%+ | Web app | Unverified Deloitte/Forrester claims |

**Caveat:** All accuracy claims are vendor self-reported and independently unvalidated [63][64].

---

## Category 2: Jira Plugins

| Plugin | Method | Key Feature | Ref |
|---|---|---|---|
| Intelligent Story Point Estimation (nVision) | ML + fuzzy matching on past Jira data | Historical pattern matching | [66] |
| EstiMate AI | Historical data-driven | One team halved planning time | [67] |
| Smart AI for Jira | ChatGPT/Azure/Gemini backends | Sprint plan generation from backlog | Marketplace |

## Category 3: AI Sprint Planning Assistants

| Tool | AI Estimation Features | Target | Ref |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zenhub | Historical Git data, predictive estimation | GitHub-first teams | [68] |
| Forecast | AI capacity planning, project learning | Professional services (25-200+) | Tools research |
| Baseliner AI | Reinforcement learning, velocity trends | Agile teams | Tools research |
| Planisware | Monte Carlo simulation, gradient-boosted regression | Fortune 500 | Tools research |
| Linear | Limited AI estimation | Modern dev teams | Tools research |

---

## Category 4: LLM-Based Estimation Research

### Anthropic JIRA Estimation Study [19]

Claude was tested on JIRA task duration estimation with only title and description:
- Claude: Spearman rho = 0.44
- Human developers (with full context): Spearman rho = 0.50
- Claude "significantly overestimates short tasks and underestimates long ones"
- Surprisingly close performance given Claude received only ticket text, not codebase context

### Claude Code Estimation Feature: Deliberately Blocked [70]

GitHub Issue #21449 requested effort estimation in Claude Code. Closed as NOT_PLANNED.
- "Claude Code is explicitly configured to refuse any time or effort estimates"
- Despite research showing Claude's estimation is comparable to developers [19]

### Valletta Software LLM Comparison [71]

Claude 3.7 outperformed GPT-4o and DeepSeek V3 for detecting requirement changes and estimating impact. Recommends "AI-generated first draft + human validation" as practical workflow.

---

## Category 5: Productivity Measurement Platforms

These measure actual AI impact, providing calibration data for future estimates:

| Platform | Method | Key Finding | Ref |
|---|---|---|---|
| DX | Survey + file-change detection | 60% active usage ceiling | [26] |
| Faros AI | Telemetry from 10K+ devs | +21% tasks but unchanged DORA | [27] |
| LinearB | PR labeling (AI vs human) | Adoption -> Impact -> ROI framework | Tools research |
| CodeScene | Behavioral code analysis | Alert-level Code Health = 15x defects, 124% more dev time | [69] |

---

## Category 6: Academic Prototypes

| Paper | Approach | Key Result | Ref |
|---|---|---|---|
| GPT2SP (IEEE TSE) | Transformer-based story point estimation | State-of-the-art baseline | [14] |
| SEEAgent (arXiv 2025) | Multi-agent consensus estimation | Outperforms ML baselines; 75% practitioner acceptance | [9] |
| Pavlic et al. (MDPI 2024) | GitLab Duo for estimation | Only 16% accuracy | [8] |
| Search-Based LLM Shots (Springer 2024) | Optimized few-shot examples for GPT-4 | Improved estimation with better examples | Academic |
| Comparative Learning (arXiv 2025) | Pairwise comparison instead of absolute points | Lower cognitive burden | Academic |

**Key limitation:** "State-of-the-art models like GPT2SP only make accurate predictions when trained on data from the same project" [academic research].

---

## The Critical Gap

The two concepts remain disconnected:
1. **"AI that estimates work"** -- tools using LLMs to predict effort (Devtimate, SEEAgent, Quanter)
2. **"Estimation that accounts for AI doing the work"** -- adjusting estimates because AI assistants change implementation speed

No tool bridges this gap. The closest proposals:
- Modified COCOMO with AAF (0.5-1.5) [48] -- not productized
- Task segmentation into AI-suitable vs non-AI-suitable [48] -- no tool implements this
- DX/Faros measurement data could calibrate estimation adjustments -- no integration exists

## Gaps and Limitations

- Vendor accuracy claims (80-95%) are universally unvalidated by independent research
- Academic estimation tools achieve much lower accuracy (16% [8]) than vendor claims
- No tool accounts for the METR finding that AI can make experienced developers slower [3]
- Measurement platforms (DX, Faros, LinearB) collect the data needed for calibration but don't feed back into estimation
- All Jira plugins assume a traditional estimation workflow; none adapt for AI-assisted implementation
